document.title: Risk Management Plan for HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management plan for HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detectors.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Establishes and documents planned risk management activities, responsibilities, risk acceptability criteria, residual risk evaluation, verification, and production/post-production information review.
keyPoints: The plan applies to all stages of the ADD life cycle.
The products are classified as IIa and have an expected service life of approximately 5 years.
Risk acceptability uses a severity multiplied by probability matrix with levels 1-4 acceptable and 5-25 unacceptable.

Post-production information is collected from internal sources such as PMCF, complaints, CAPA, service records, and surveys, and external sources such as recalls, incidents, literature, similar devices, and laws or standards.

concepts.name: Risk management plan
concepts.evidence: This plan includes the following: the scope of the planned risk management activities... assignment of responsibilities and authorities... criteria for risk acceptability...

concepts.name: Risk acceptability criteria
concepts.evidence: Criteria for Risk Acceptability Two risk regions - The combination of the probability and severity of the matrix approach is used.

concepts.name: Overall residual risk evaluation
concepts.evidence: After all risk control measures have been implemented and verified, the manufacturer shall evaluate the overall residual risk posed by the medical device...

concepts.name: Production and post-production information
concepts.evidence: Activities Related to Collection and Review of Relevant Production and post-production information

concepts.name: Essential performance
concepts.evidence: Essential performances Manufacturer shall identify which functions are Essential Performance.

concepts.name: Intended purpose
concepts.evidence: The ADD is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The document is a Risk Management Plan for HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detectors.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management Plan for HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The product name is Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The model names are HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
The document revision is 0 and the revision date is Sep/15/2023.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision No. 0 Rev. Date Sep/15/2023 Revision Description Initial

evidence.claim: The plan applies to all stages of the ADD life cycle.
evidence.evidenceText: This Risk management plan is applicable to all stages of the life cycle of the ADD.

evidence.claim: The ADD is described as wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors for digital radiography systems.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD is wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors that have been designed for faster, more streamlined approach to digital radiography systems.

evidence.claim: The device classification listed in the plan is IIa.
The expected service life is approximately 5 years.
The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, not for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children... Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The applicable European regulation listed is Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017

evidence.claim: The plan references Medical Device Act in Korea and implementing regulation in Korea for the risk management report.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk management report Medical Device Act in Korea Implementing regulation in medical device in Korea

evidence.claim: Risk acceptability criteria are based on applicable national or regional regulations, relevant International Standards, state of the art, and stakeholder concerns.
evidence.evidenceText: This policy shall ensure that criteria are based upon applicable national or regional regulations and relevant International Standards, and take into account available information such as the generally accepted state of the art and known stakeholder concerns.

evidence.claim: Risk level is calculated as severity rating multiplied by probability rating.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk level = Severity rating × Probability rating

evidence.claim: Estimated risk levels 1-4 are acceptable and 5-25 are unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Level: 1~4 Green, Acceptable Risk (A) Acceptable region Risk Level: 5~25 Red, Unacceptable Risk (U) Unacceptable region

evidence.claim: Overall residual risk is evaluated after all risk control measures have been implemented and verified.
evidence.evidenceText: After all risk control measures have been implemented and verified, the manufacturer shall evaluate the overall residual risk posed by the medical device...

evidence.claim: Post-production information is collected from internal and external information sources.
evidence.evidenceText: The get information method of post-production information should be collected according to the internal information and the external information.

evidence.claim: The Quality manager reports safety information and side effects to the regulatory agency when necessary.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the seriousness of the information collected, the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency (Applicable national or regional regulations).

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text cites European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and Korean medical device regulations; FDA is not evidenced in the source text.
The source text includes OCR or extraction quality issues such as broken spacing, grammar errors, and table formatting loss.
Manufacturer identity is inferred from the sentence naming H&Abyz as the organization establishing the plan; no formal manufacturer field is present.
